name: Sweet Carrot Teriyaki Pasta Salad with Napa and Portabellas
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups shredded cabbage (napa or bok choy)
recipeInstructions:
1. Process carrots and water until well grated. Add teriyaki sauce and blend.
2. Spray a wok with non-stick cooking spray over medium heat. Add minced garlic and diced Portabella mushrooms; toss until softened (about 4-5 minutes). Sprinkle with cracked pepper.
3. Add roasted bell pepper and stir. If needed, add oil.
4. Stir in shredded cabbage for about 3 minutes.
5. Make a well in the center of the wok and add the carrot mixture.
6. Heat gently until warm.
7. Incorporate cooked pasta and toss with mushroom vegetables from the rim of the wok. Add crisp wontons if desired.
8. Serve cold as pasta salad or hot/warm as light entree or side dish.
Nutrition:
Without wontons: 260 calories, 1.4g fat, 4.7% CFF (mc).
With chicken added: 351 calories, 4.9g fat (12.8% cff).
Notes:
This is the base salad. Additional chopped vegetables can be added such as steamed artichoke hearts, baby corn, blanched broccoli, slivered and steamed fennel bulb, pea pods or snap peas, hearts of palm.
Teriyaki: Use Mr. Yasida's gourmet sauce, Sagawa, or Ibarre. Substitute soy sauce sweetened with brown sugar and citrus juice. Serve with seafood, fish, chicken, grilled pork, tofu cubes, or additional vegetables.
